name: Route of the Faces and Buendía Reservoir — circular from Buendía
name_original: Ruta de la Caras y Embalse de Buendía — circular desde Buendía
region_intro: No traffic touring cycling routes around Embalse de Buendía traverse a rugged and rocky landscape, characterized by hills and mountains covered with dense forests of oak and pine trees. The region features numerous cliffs and gorges, offering dramatic vistas over the clear blue waters of the reservoir. Cyclists can expect to encounter impressive rock formations and scenic viewpoints, particularly around the reservoir's varied terrain. Best no traffic touring cycling routes around Embalse de Buendía The most popular no traffic touring cycling route is Castejón — circular from Cañaveruelas, a 24.1 miles (38.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 4 minutes to complete. This route offers varied terrain through the natural landscape surrounding the reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Route of the Faces loop from Buendía, an easy 6.6 miles (10.6 km) path. This trail leads through a pine forest adjacent to the reservoir, featuring unique rock sculptures. Local touring cyclists also love the Giralda de Escamilla loop from Alcocer, a 21.6 miles (34.8 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 59 minutes. Touring cycling around Embalse de Buendía is defined by rugged landscapes, dense pine forests, and the expansive clear waters of the reservoir. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging routes with significant elevation. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 40 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Embalse de Buendía's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many no-traffic touring cycling routes are available around Embalse de Buendía?
A: There are 5 dedicated no-traffic touring cycling routes detailed in this guide, ranging from easy to difficult. These routes allow you to explore the diverse landscapes of Embalse de Buendía without vehicle interference.

Q: Are there any easy no-traffic touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, for an easy and family-friendly option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31428678" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Faces loop from Buendía&lt;/a&gt;. This 10.6 km route is perfect for a relaxed ride and includes the unique open-air art installation of the Ruta de las Caras, featuring sculptures carved into sandstone rocks.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect on these no-traffic touring cycling routes?
A: The routes around Embalse de Buendía traverse a rugged and rocky landscape, featuring hills and mountains covered with dense forests of oak and pine trees. You'll encounter numerous cliffs, gorges, and impressive rock formations, all set against the clear blue waters of the reservoir. Aromatic herbs like rosemary, sage, and thyme also contribute to the natural ambiance.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints or landmarks along the no-traffic cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. Many routes offer stunning vistas. A notable highlight is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5398646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña de la Virgen Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, which provides expansive panoramic views of the reservoir. You can also see the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3906128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buendía Dam&lt;/a&gt;, a significant man-made landmark.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Embalse de Buendía?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally ideal for touring cycling in Embalse de Buendía. The weather is milder, and the natural scenery is particularly vibrant, with blooming wildflowers in spring and rich colors in autumn. Summers can be hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are recommended if cycling during this season.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging no-traffic touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, for those seeking a more challenging ride, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31458068" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Giralda de Escamilla loop from Alcocer&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 34.8 km route with significant elevation changes. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21952040" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castejón loop from Cañaveruelas&lt;/a&gt;, spanning 33 km with substantial climbs.

Q: Can I find circular no-traffic touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, all the no-traffic touring cycling routes featured in this guide are circular, allowing you to start and end your journey in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1095106500/castejon-circular-from-canaveruelas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castejón — circular from Cañaveruelas&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31456898" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike loop from Sacedón&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical sites can be explored near the no-traffic cycling routes?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can explore historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5440124" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of La Isabela Spa&lt;/a&gt;, a former spa town that was submerged when the reservoir was built and is occasionally visible during low water levels. The nearby town of Buendía also boasts a Gothic-style church and remnants of a 15th-century medieval wall.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about the no-traffic routes in Embalse de Buendía?
A: Cyclists frequently praise the tranquility and natural beauty of the no-traffic routes around Embalse de Buendía. The absence of vehicle traffic allows for a peaceful and immersive experience, with many appreciating the stunning reservoir views, the diverse forest landscapes, and the unique cultural points of interest like the Ruta de las Caras.

Q: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the no-traffic cycling routes?
A: The towns and villages surrounding Embalse de Buendía, such as Buendía, Cañaveruelas, Alcocer, and Sacedón, offer various amenities. You can find local cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options to refuel and rest before or after your ride. It's advisable to check opening hours, especially during off-peak seasons.
tips: Located near the town in Cuenca that gives it its name, it was inaugurated in 1958 at a height of 78.10 m and with a water reservoir capacity of 1,638 hm³ in an area of 8,194 ha. Its waters bathe, among others, the towns of Buendía, Alcocer, Alcohujate, Villalba del Rey. From it, you can access the route of the Hermitage of the Virgen de los Desamparados 'Favorite Site', which is worth visiting.

The Buendía reservoir is located on the border between the provinces of Cuenca and Guadalajara and is one of the most important in the community of Castilla La Mancha. On the route of the faces is this viewpoint with beautiful views towards it.

Free access tourist route located near the municipality of Buendía, Cuenca, among pine forests, sandstone abounds. In them you will find the Route of the Faces, a hiking route of a couple of kilometers in which you will see yourself accompanied by pine trees and allegorical sculptures.
Faces sculpted in sandstone rock of different sizes, some spectacular, with dimensions of four and six meters, which took eight years to sculpt due to the hardness of the rock.
http://www.rutadelascaras.com/
